articles.title: Report: Antetokounmpo to join Miami Heat in blockbuster NBA trade
articles.description: Greek basketball superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo is heading from the Milwaukee Bucks to the Miami Heat in a blockbuster NBA trade, ESPN reported, citing sources. The 31-year-old has spent his entire NBA career with the Bucks since joining the league in 2013 and is widely regarded as one of the best basketball players in the world.

articles.title: Wolves Trade Julius Randle To Nets; Bulls Acquire Nic Claxton
articles.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have traded Julius Randle to the Brooklyn Nets as part of a three-team trade with the Chicago Bulls. The Bulls will acquire Nic Claxton from the Nets as part of the deal. Minnesota will also send the No. 28 pick to the Nets while getting the No. 33 pick back in return. The Wolves re-signed Randle during the 2025 offseason on a three-year, $100 million contract as they prioritized retaining him over Nickeil Alexander-Walker. Minnesota is now unloading Randle as they look to re-sign Ayo Dosunmu and open up the use of their exceptions. Randle appeared in 79 games last season, averaging 21.1 points, 6.7 rebounds and 5.0 assists in 33.0 minutes. Randle's 18.2 PER in the regular season dropped to 10.9 during the playoffs.

articles.title: Argentina downs Austria, Messi sets World Cup scoring record
articles.description: ARLINGTON, United States — Lionel Messi became the leading scorer in World Cup history as the captain struck twice to give Argentina a 2-0 win over Austria on Monday and send the champions into the last 32. The player widely regarded as the greatest of all time pounced late in the first half in Texas with a trademark left-footed finish after neat build-up play. The predatory goal added to his hat-trick in Argentina’s opening match to make it a record 17 in total at the World Cup. READ: Magical Messi equals World Cup goals record as Argentina wins The 38-year-old [...]... Keep on reading: Argentina downs Austria, Messi sets World Cup scoring record
